slides.title: The workout that *spares your joints*.
slides.sub: Water carries most of your weight, so aching joints can move freely. Here is why swimming earns a place in a longevity routine.

slides.title: Buoyancy unloads the joints
slides.body: Standing in chest-deep water cuts the load on your knees and hips dramatically. Sore, arthritic joints *can move freely* again.

slides.title: Full range, low impact
slides.body: Water removes the pounding, not the effort. You can train through a *complete range of motion* without the jarring of pavement.

slides.title: Heart and lungs still work
slides.body: Swimming keeps demanding oxygen, so it *can support* the VO2 max that tracks closely with a long, capable life.

slides.title: Water is resistance
slides.body: Every stroke pushes against the water from all sides. That steady resistance *can help* preserve real muscular strength.

slides.title: Gentle, then build
slides.body: Begin with easy laps or water walking, then add pace as your joints allow. *Consistency* matters more than speed.

slides.title: Make every pool day *count*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your swims, steps, sleep and food, then folds them into one daily Health Score so you can see the gains add up.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Aching joints don't mean the end of hard training. The water just carries the weight for you. 🌊🌿

Buoyancy unloads arthritic knees and hips while swimming still protects your VO2 max and full-range strength. Save this for your next pool day. 🔖

Vibe Health turns every swim into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Swimming Is the Best Workout for Aging Joints
